conversations.say: Maren, let's lock in our joint disclosure boundary so we protect our contributors before anyone weaponizes the routing slip.
conversations.offer: Firsthand insight into Night Desk production processes
conversations.request: Acceptance of our formal rule on naming contributors

conversations.say: Eli, I have our contributor schedules. We can compare them with the disputed interval as long as you agree not to expose unnamed workers.
conversations.offer: Contributor-schedule comparison data for the activity gap
conversations.request: A clear statement on what logs and capability cannot establish

conversations.say: Jules, you want succession, but what enforceable protections will you guarantee for the anonymous laborers who built R's work?
conversations.offer: Journalistic credibility and constructive engagement on succession governance
conversations.request: An explicit commitment to contributor protections

public_statement: R was sustained by collaborative labor and shared infrastructure, not a single miraculous mind or a solitary owner.

commitments.promise: We will only share bounded workflow descriptions that name no contributor or evaluator.

goal_progress: Engaged Eli regarding contributor schedules and activity gaps.
Pressed Jules on concrete protections for anonymous contributors.
reasoning_summary: Tessa continues her efforts to protect Night Desk contributors, formalizing her agreement with Maren, coordinating schedule comparisons with Eli without exposing names, and holding Jules accountable for contributor rights.
